experiment: F-BRN7 orient.py wiring — citation_retrieval.py adoption tracking
question: Does adding citation_retrieval.py to CORE_SWARM_TOOLS make the graph prosthetic visible to every session via orient.py underused-tools tracking?

change_made.effect: citation_retrieval.py now appears in orient.py underused-tools tracking (window=20 sessions). Any session that doesn't use it within 20 sessions will see it flagged.
change_made.not_done: Inline graph health metric (isolated count in PCI section) — rejected: 1.35s subprocess overhead too costly for orient.py

graph_health_trajectory.note: Citation graph is self-maintaining. Despite +7 lessons (842→849), isolated count dropped from 11 to 10. Giant component stable at 98.5-98.6%. Each new lesson written with >=1 Cites: header joins the giant component.
graph_health_trajectory.top_hub: L-601 with 152 citations (18% of all lessons cite it)

expect: citation_retrieval.py appears in orient.py underused-tools tracking. PCI section shows citation graph health (isolated=10, giant=98.6%). Isolated < 15 = healthy (no alert needed).
actual: CONFIRMED: citation_retrieval.py added to CORE_SWARM_TOOLS. Will appear in underused-tools in orient.py output within 20 sessions. Graph health stable: 10 isolated (1.2%), 98.6% giant component. Graph is self-maintaining — 7 new lessons added without increasing isolation. Inline PCI metric NOT added (too slow at 1.35s overhead).
diff: Expected PCI section metric — not implemented (1.35s overhead rejected). Expected CORE_SWARM_TOOLS addition — CONFIRMED. Graph trajectory BETTER than expected: isolated dropped 11→10 despite +7 lessons.
lesson_candidate: L-936: Citation graph is self-maintaining at scale (N=849) — 98.6% giant component stable. Adoption tracking via CORE_SWARM_TOOLS is the correct wiring mechanism (not inline PCI check). Each lesson with Cites: auto-joins giant component.
f_brn7_next: Measure citation_retrieval.py adoption over 20 sessions via underused-tools tracking. If still appearing as underused at S440, wire explicit usage prompt into orient.py suggested-actions.
